channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Centre makes key appointments in NTA amid NEET-UG 2026 paper leak investigation
item.description: New Delhi: The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet has sanctioned significant appointments in the National Testing Agency (NTA) under the Department of Higher Education, as the NEET-UG 2026 paper le...

item.title: Closing the Retirement Savings Gap for South Africa’s Domestic Workers
item.description: Despite recent legal reforms in South Africa’s domestic work sector, domestic workers remain largely excluded from retirement savings. Reforms have mandated registration for the UIF for anyone working...

item.title: Carlton secures thrilling win over Western Bulldogs with standout performances from Cripps and Ison
item.description: Carlton delivered a stirring performance against the Western Bulldogs at the MCG, marking the start of the post-Michael Voss era with a 12-point win, 10.14 (74) to 9.8 (62). Patrick Cripps led the cha...

item.title: APC Don Announce List Of House Of Reps Aspirants Wey Dem Disqualified
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don release di names of aspirants wey dem no clear to contest for House of Representatives primaries ahead of 2027 general elections. Dis announcement cover aspiran...

item.title: Court to Examine Delays in South Africa’s Firearms Registration System
item.description: The Pretoria High Court is set to scrutinise the Central Firearms Registry and a proposed electronic system linking police with firearms industry bodies. The South African Arms and Ammunition Dealers’...

item.title: Hearts fall short on final day but deliver a remarkable season
item.description: Hearts suffered heartbreak once again on the final day of the Scottish Premiership, missing out on the title despite leading the league for 250 days. The dramatic conclusion saw Celtic clinch victory ...

item.title: ATCL to Begin Direct Flights Between Tanzania and Moscow to Boost Tourism and Trade
item.description: Air Tanzania Company Limited (ATCL) is planning to start direct flights connecting Dar es Salaam and Zanzibar with Moscow before the end of this year. The flights will go via Seychelles using ATCL's B...

item.title: LelQuin Teams Up with Songbayanang Choir to Perform 'Singko Blanko' on Everybody Sing
item.description: On May 16, 2026, ABS-CBN Studios highlighted a special musical performance on their popular show Everybody Sing, featuring LelQuin alongside the Songbayanang Choir. In this segment, the talented duo c...

item.title: Julius Malema Faces Legal Challenges Amid EFF Leadership Uncertainty
item.description: Julius Malema, leader of South Africa's fourth-largest party, the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF), finds himself at a critical juncture. In April 2026, a lower court convicted him of illegal possessio...

item.title: Large Crowds Attend Far-Right and Counter-Protests in London Amid Tight Security
item.description: On Saturday, London witnessed a massive turnout for two opposing events. Far-right activist Tommy Robinson organised his 'Unite the Kingdom' march, reportedly drawing around 50,000 participants, while...

item.title: Exploring the Life and Drawings of Paula Rego at Victoria Miro
item.description: Paula Rego’s latest exhibition, Story Line, at the Victoria Miro gallery in London, presents the most comprehensive display of her drawings to date. Born in Lisbon in 1935, Rego lived under Portugal’s...

item.title: Boston man dies in escalator accident at subway station
item.description: Steven McCluskey, a 40-year-old carpenter and father of two, tragically died in a subway station accident in Boston. On 27 February, while descending an escalator at Davis Station in Somerville, he lo...

item.title: Oil prices surge as Middle East supply disruptions and Strait of Hormuz closure spark market volatility
item.description: Global oil prices surged on Friday as ongoing supply disruptions in the Middle East coincided with a lack of significant progress at the high-level summit in Beijing between US President Donald Trump ...

item.title: Pakistan's Interior Minister Visits Tehran to Support US-Iran Peace Efforts
item.description: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi arrived in Tehran on Saturday for an official two-day visit aimed at supporting and facilitating the ongoing peace talks between Iran and the United States, w...

item.title: Young Man Narrates Regret After Friend Dies in a Fight Over Alcohol
item.description: Alvin, a young man from Kiruavunjo West in the Kilimanjaro region, broke down in tears as he narrated the events that led to the death of his friend. The incident reportedly started at a drinking spot...

item.title: Regenerative Agriculture Becomes Crucial for South African Farmers Amid Climate and Supply Challenges
item.description: Farmers in South Africa are increasingly recognising that sustainable and regenerative farming practices are no longer optional but essential for long-term profitability. At the Nampo Harvest Day even...

item.title: US Congressman Appreciates Pakistan's Leadership in Facilitating US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: In a recent development, US Congressman Jack Bergman, Co-Chair of the Congressional Pakistan Caucus, has formally expressed his gratitude to Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of Defence Forces F...

item.title: Myanmar military restricts menstrual products amid civil war, exacerbating hardship for women
item.description: The military government in Myanmar has reportedly expanded restrictions on the transport of menstrual products across key routes as part of its ongoing civil war. This move appears to extend the Tatma...

item.title: Inside Peridot Bar: A Green Gem-Inspired Lounge in Hong Kong
item.description: Perched on the 38th floor of The Henderson in Hong Kong, Peridot Bar is a visually striking cocktail lounge designed by Studio Paolo Ferrari. Wrapped almost entirely in soft emerald plaster, the inter...

item.title: NGCP Lifts Red and Yellow Power Alerts as Supply Stabilizes in Luzon and Visayas
item.description: The National Grid Corporation of the Philippines (NGCP) announced on May 16, 2026, that red and yellow power alerts in Luzon and Visayas have been lifted. These alerts were initially raised due to the...
